newsletters.subject: Import AI 463: Self-improving robots; a 10k Chinese GPU cluster; and an elegiac essay for the human era
newsletters.rationale: Jack Clark's Import AI is a top-tier AI policy/tech newsletter. Topics: self-improving robots (robotics + AI agents), 10k Chinese GPU cluster (compute geopolitics, AI infrastructure), elegiac essay for human era (AI safety/alignment philosophy). All three themes directly relevant to wiki coverage areas.
newsletters.notes: Substack redirect links — need to resolve actual article URLs from the read_url. High signal newsletter from Anthropic co-founder.

newsletters.subject: 🎙️ How I AI: GLM-5.2 review & How Gusto built a new product line with Claude Code
newsletters.rationale: Two substantive topics: (1) GLM-5.2 review — Zhipu AI's latest model, relevant to China AI and model comparisons; (2) Gusto case study of Claude Code in production — real-world coding agent adoption story. Both directly relevant to wiki entity/concept pages.
newsletters.notes: GLM-5.2 may need entity/concept page. Gusto Claude Code adoption is a valuable case study for coding-agents concept page.

newsletters.subject: Monday Template Title
newsletters.rationale: Title is literally 'Monday Template Title' — appears to be a test/template email or misconfigured newsletter. All links are opaque beehiiv redirects with no discernible content. Not worth scraping.
newsletters.notes: Likely a test/template newsletter. No actionable content from subject line alone. Beehiiv links are heavily encoded and may not resolve cleanly.

newsletters.subject: [AINews] not much happened today
newsletters.rationale: swyx's AINews is a curated AI community digest. Despite the 'not much happened' title, these digests typically contain HN/Reddit-sourced AI discussions and links. The substack redirect links (link 8-20) likely contain the actual curated article links. Worth scraping the main post to check for relevant content.
newsletters.notes: swyx's AINews aggregates community discussions. Low signal from title alone but may contain useful links. Process after high-priority items.
